channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2025 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Superfluid Stiffness Bounds in Time-Reversal Symmetric Superconductors
item.description:
Author(s): Yongxin Zeng and Andrew J. Millis

Quantum geometry has been shown to make an important contribution to the superfluid stiffness of flatband superconductors including moiré materials. In this Letter we use mean-field theory to derive an expression for the superfluid stiffness of time-reversal symmetric superconductors at zero tempera…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266004] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yongxin Zeng and Andrew J. Millis

Quantum geometry has been shown to make an important contribution to the superfluid stiffness of flatband superconductors including moiré materials. In this Letter we use mean-field theory to derive an expression for the superfluid stiffness of time-reversal symmetric superconductors at zero tempera…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266004] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.dc:title: Superfluid Stiffness Bounds in Time-Reversal Symmetric Superconductors
item.dc:creator: Yongxin Zeng and Andrew J. Millis
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Experimental Measurement of the Reappearance of Rabi Rotations in Semiconductor Quantum Dots
item.description:
Author(s): Lukas Hanschke, Thomas K. Bracht, Eva Schöll, David Bauch, Eva Berger, Patricia Kallert, Melina Peter, Ailton J. Garcia, Jr., Saimon F. Covre da Silva, Santanu Manna, Armando Rastelli, Stefan Schumacher, Doris E. Reiter, and Klaus D. Jöns

Phonons in solid-state quantum emitters play a crucial role in their performance as photon sources in quantum technology. For resonant driving, phonons dampen the Rabi oscillations, resulting in reduced state-preparation fidelities. The phonon spectral density, which quantifies the strength of the c…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 263602] Published Fri Dec 26, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Lukas Hanschke, Thomas K. Bracht, Eva Schöll, David Bauch, Eva Berger, Patricia Kallert, Melina Peter, Ailton J. Garcia, Jr., Saimon F. Covre da Silva, Santanu Manna, Armando Rastelli, Stefan Schumacher, Doris E. Reiter, and Klaus D. Jöns

Phonons in solid-state quantum emitters play a crucial role in their performance as photon sources in quantum technology. For resonant driving, phonons dampen the Rabi oscillations, resulting in reduced state-preparation fidelities. The phonon spectral density, which quantifies the strength of the c…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 263602] Published Fri Dec 26, 2025
item.dc:title: Experimental Measurement of the Reappearance of Rabi Rotations in Semiconductor Quantum Dots
item.dc:creator: Lukas Hanschke, Thomas K. Bracht, Eva Schöll, David Bauch, Eva Berger, Patricia Kallert, Melina Peter, Ailton J. Garcia, Jr., Saimon F. Covre da Silva, Santanu Manna, Armando Rastelli, Stefan Schumacher, Doris E. Reiter, and Klaus D. Jöns
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Symmetry Classification for Alternating Excitons in Two-Dimensional Altermagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Jiayu David Cao (曹嘉宇), Konstantin S. Denisov, Yuntian Liu (刘云天), and Igor Žutić

Excitons, bound electron-hole states, often dominate the optical response of two-dimensional (2D) materials and reflect their inherent properties, including spin-orbit coupling, magnetic ordering, or band topology. By focusing on a growing class of collinear antiferromagnets with a nonrelativistic s…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266703] Published Fri Dec 26, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiayu David Cao (曹嘉宇), Konstantin S. Denisov, Yuntian Liu (刘云天), and Igor Žutić

Excitons, bound electron-hole states, often dominate the optical response of two-dimensional (2D) materials and reflect their inherent properties, including spin-orbit coupling, magnetic ordering, or band topology. By focusing on a growing class of collinear antiferromagnets with a nonrelativistic s…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266703] Published Fri Dec 26, 2025
item.dc:title: Symmetry Classification for Alternating Excitons in Two-Dimensional Altermagnets
item.dc:creator: Jiayu David Cao (曹嘉宇), Konstantin S. Denisov, Yuntian Liu (刘云天), and Igor Žutić
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Spin Decoherence Dynamics of ${\mathrm{Er}}^{3+}$ in ${\mathrm{CeO}}_{2}$ Films
item.description:
Author(s): Sagar Kumar Seth, Jonah Nagura, Vrindaa Somjit, Aneesh Bapat, Xinhao Li, Gregory D. Grant, Ignas Masiulionis, Xu Han, F. Joseph Heremans, Giulia Galli, David D. Awschalom, Supratik Guha, and Jiefei Zhang

Developing telecom-compatible spin-photon interfaces is essential towards scalable quantum networks. Erbium ions (${\mathrm{Er}}^{3+}$) exhibit a unique combination of a telecom ($1.5\text{ }\text{ }\mathrm{μ}\mathrm{m}$) optical transition and an effective spin-$1/2$ ground state, but identifying a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266901] Published Fri Dec 26, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sagar Kumar Seth, Jonah Nagura, Vrindaa Somjit, Aneesh Bapat, Xinhao Li, Gregory D. Grant, Ignas Masiulionis, Xu Han, F. Joseph Heremans, Giulia Galli, David D. Awschalom, Supratik Guha, and Jiefei Zhang

Developing telecom-compatible spin-photon interfaces is essential towards scalable quantum networks. Erbium ions (Er3+) exhibit a unique combination of a telecom (1.5  μm) optical transition and an effective spin-1/2 ground state, but identifying a host that enables heterogeneous device integration …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266901] Published Fri Dec 26, 2025
item.dc:title: Spin Decoherence Dynamics of ${\mathrm{Er}}^{3+}$ in ${\mathrm{CeO}}_{2}$ Films
item.dc:creator: Sagar Kumar Seth, Jonah Nagura, Vrindaa Somjit, Aneesh Bapat, Xinhao Li, Gregory D. Grant, Ignas Masiulionis, Xu Han, F. Joseph Heremans, Giulia Galli, David D. Awschalom, Supratik Guha, and Jiefei Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Multiscale Explanation of the Missing Gallium Vacancy in Gallium Arsenide
item.description:
Author(s): Leopoldo Diaz, Harold P. Hjalmarson, Jesse J. Lutz, and Peter A. Schultz

Irradiation of gallium arsenide (GaAs) produces immobile vacancies and mobile interstitials. Yet, after decades of experimental investigation, the immobile Ga vacancy continues to evade detection, raising the question: where is the Ga vacancy? Static first-principles calculations predict a Ga vacanc…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 267001] Published Fri Dec 26, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Leopoldo Diaz, Harold P. Hjalmarson, Jesse J. Lutz, and Peter A. Schultz

Irradiation of gallium arsenide (GaAs) produces immobile vacancies and mobile interstitials. Yet, after decades of experimental investigation, the immobile Ga vacancy continues to evade detection, raising the question: where is the Ga vacancy? Static first-principles calculations predict a Ga vacanc…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 267001] Published Fri Dec 26, 2025
item.dc:title: Multiscale Explanation of the Missing Gallium Vacancy in Gallium Arsenide
item.dc:creator: Leopoldo Diaz, Harold P. Hjalmarson, Jesse J. Lutz, and Peter A. Schultz
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Time-Domain Extreme-Ultraviolet Diffuse Scattering Spectroscopy of Nanoscale Surface Phonons
item.description:
Author(s): F. Capotondi, A. A. Maznev, F. Bencivenga, S. Bonetti, D. Engel, D. Fainozzi, D. Fausti, L. Foglia, C. Gutt, N. Jaouen, D. Ksenzov, C. Masciovecchio, Keith A. Nelson, I. Nikolov, M. Pancaldi, E. Pedersoli, B. Pfau, L. Raimondi, F. Romanelli, R. Totani, and M. Trigo

Imaging using extreme ultraviolet scattering shows that optical pulses can generate surface excitations with spectra that were previously difficult to achieve.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266101] Published Wed Dec 24, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): F. Capotondi, A. A. Maznev, F. Bencivenga, S. Bonetti, D. Engel, D. Fainozzi, D. Fausti, L. Foglia, C. Gutt, N. Jaouen, D. Ksenzov, C. Masciovecchio, Keith A. Nelson, I. Nikolov, M. Pancaldi, E. Pedersoli, B. Pfau, L. Raimondi, F. Romanelli, R. Totani, and M. Trigo

Imaging using extreme ultraviolet scattering shows that optical pulses can generate surface excitations with spectra that were previously difficult to achieve.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266101] Published Wed Dec 24, 2025
item.dc:title: Time-Domain Extreme-Ultraviolet Diffuse Scattering Spectroscopy of Nanoscale Surface Phonons
item.dc:creator: F. Capotondi, A. A. Maznev, F. Bencivenga, S. Bonetti, D. Engel, D. Fainozzi, D. Fausti, L. Foglia, C. Gutt, N. Jaouen, D. Ksenzov, C. Masciovecchio, Keith A. Nelson, I. Nikolov, M. Pancaldi, E. Pedersoli, B. Pfau, L. Raimondi, F. Romanelli, R. Totani, and M. Trigo

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Itinerant to Localized Heavy Electron Magnetism in $\mathrm{Ce}({\mathrm{Ru}}_{1−x}{\mathrm{Rh}}_{x}{)}_{2}{\mathrm{Al}}_{10}$: A Direction-Dependent Phase Diagram beyond the Doniach Phase Diagram
item.description:
Author(s): Hitoshi Yamaoka, Hiroshi Tanida, Eike F. Schwier, Yoshiya Yamamoto, Shiv Kumar, Masashi Arita, Kenya Shimada, Fumisato Tajima, Renta Onodera, Takashi Nishioka, Hirofumi Ishii, Nozomu Hiraoka, and Jun’ichiro Mizuki

Electronic and crystal structures of $\mathrm{Ce}({\mathrm{Ru}}_{1−x}{\mathrm{Rh}}_{x}{)}_{2}{\mathrm{Al}}_{10}$ have been studied using x-ray emission spectroscopy, photoelectron spectroscopy (PES), and x-ray diffraction. No structural phase transition was observed up to $x=0.1$, while the x-ray ab…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266503] Published Wed Dec 24, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hitoshi Yamaoka, Hiroshi Tanida, Eike F. Schwier, Yoshiya Yamamoto, Shiv Kumar, Masashi Arita, Kenya Shimada, Fumisato Tajima, Renta Onodera, Takashi Nishioka, Hirofumi Ishii, Nozomu Hiraoka, and Jun’ichiro Mizuki

Electronic and crystal structures of Ce(Ru1−xRhx)2Al10 have been studied using x-ray emission spectroscopy, photoelectron spectroscopy (PES), and x-ray diffraction. No structural phase transition was observed up to x=0.1, while the x-ray absorption spectra showed a Ce valence transition between x=0.…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266503] Published Wed Dec 24, 2025
item.dc:title: Itinerant to Localized Heavy Electron Magnetism in $\mathrm{Ce}({\mathrm{Ru}}_{1−x}{\mathrm{Rh}}_{x}{)}_{2}{\mathrm{Al}}_{10}$: A Direction-Dependent Phase Diagram beyond the Doniach Phase Diagram
item.dc:creator: Hitoshi Yamaoka, Hiroshi Tanida, Eike F. Schwier, Yoshiya Yamamoto, Shiv Kumar, Masashi Arita, Kenya Shimada, Fumisato Tajima, Renta Onodera, Takashi Nishioka, Hirofumi Ishii, Nozomu Hiraoka, and Jun’ichiro Mizuki
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Circular Dichroism on the Edge of Quantum Hall Systems: From Many-Body Chern Number to Anisotropy Measurements
item.description:
Author(s): F. Nur Ünal, A. Nardin, and N. Goldman

Quantum Hall states are characterized by a topological invariant, the many-body Chern number, which determines their quantized Hall conductivity. This invariant also emerges in circular dichroic responses, namely, by applying a circular drive and comparing excitation rates for opposite orientations.…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266603] Published Wed Dec 24, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): F. Nur Ünal, A. Nardin, and N. Goldman

Quantum Hall states are characterized by a topological invariant, the many-body Chern number, which determines their quantized Hall conductivity. This invariant also emerges in circular dichroic responses, namely, by applying a circular drive and comparing excitation rates for opposite orientations.…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266603] Published Wed Dec 24, 2025
item.dc:title: Circular Dichroism on the Edge of Quantum Hall Systems: From Many-Body Chern Number to Anisotropy Measurements
item.dc:creator: F. Nur Ünal, A. Nardin, and N. Goldman
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Band Topology and Dynamic Multiferroicity Induced from Dynamical Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya Interactions in Centrosymmetric Lattices
item.description:
Author(s): Bowen Ma and Z. D. Wang

We develop a theory of a dynamical Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction (dDMI) in centrosymmetric crystals by generally considering the vibration of both cations and anions. It gives rise to an antisymmetric spin-lattice coupling, inducing magnon-phonon hybridized topological excitations. Moreover, we …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266702] Published Wed Dec 24, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bowen Ma and Z. D. Wang

We develop a theory of a dynamical Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction (dDMI) in centrosymmetric crystals by generally considering the vibration of both cations and anions. It gives rise to an antisymmetric spin-lattice coupling, inducing magnon-phonon hybridized topological excitations. Moreover, we …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266702] Published Wed Dec 24, 2025
item.dc:title: Band Topology and Dynamic Multiferroicity Induced from Dynamical Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya Interactions in Centrosymmetric Lattices
item.dc:creator: Bowen Ma and Z. D. Wang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Fast Hydrogen Atom Diffraction through Monocrystalline Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Pierre Guichard, Arnaud Dochain, Raphaël Marion, Pauline de Crombrugghe de Picquendaele, Nicolas Lejeune, Benoît Hackens, Paul-Antoine Hervieux, and Xavier Urbain

A fast beam of hydrogen atoms passes through a graphene layer and produces a diffraction pattern, a development that could lead to a new probing technique of surface interactions.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 263403] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pierre Guichard, Arnaud Dochain, Raphaël Marion, Pauline de Crombrugghe de Picquendaele, Nicolas Lejeune, Benoît Hackens, Paul-Antoine Hervieux, and Xavier Urbain

A fast beam of hydrogen atoms passes through a graphene layer and produces a diffraction pattern, a development that could lead to a new probing technique of surface interactions.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 263403] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.dc:title: Fast Hydrogen Atom Diffraction through Monocrystalline Graphene
item.dc:creator: Pierre Guichard, Arnaud Dochain, Raphaël Marion, Pauline de Crombrugghe de Picquendaele, Nicolas Lejeune, Benoît Hackens, Paul-Antoine Hervieux, and Xavier Urbain
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Spin-Depairing-Induced Exceptional Fermionic Superfluidity
item.description:
Author(s): Soma Takemori, Kazuki Yamamoto, and Akihisa Koga

We investigate the non-Hermitian (NH) attractive Hubbard model with spin depairing, which is a spin-resolved asymmetric hopping that nonreciprocally operates spins in the opposite direction. We find that spin depairing stabilizes a superfluid state unique to the NH system. This phase is characterize…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266002] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Soma Takemori, Kazuki Yamamoto, and Akihisa Koga

We investigate the non-Hermitian (NH) attractive Hubbard model with spin depairing, which is a spin-resolved asymmetric hopping that nonreciprocally operates spins in the opposite direction. We find that spin depairing stabilizes a superfluid state unique to the NH system. This phase is characterize…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266002] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.dc:title: Spin-Depairing-Induced Exceptional Fermionic Superfluidity
item.dc:creator: Soma Takemori, Kazuki Yamamoto, and Akihisa Koga
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Intrinsic Heavy Wigner Crystal Forged by Transferred $4f$ Electrons
item.description:
Author(s): Zhongjie Wang, Rui Song, Yupeng Jiang, Qidong Sun, Meng Zhao, Lifeng Yin, Jian Shen, and Chunlei Gao

A new type of Wigner crystal made of charge-transfer 4f electrons has been observed.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266502] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhongjie Wang, Rui Song, Yupeng Jiang, Qidong Sun, Meng Zhao, Lifeng Yin, Jian Shen, and Chunlei Gao

A new type of Wigner crystal made of charge-transfer 4f electrons has been observed.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266502] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.dc:title: Intrinsic Heavy Wigner Crystal Forged by Transferred $4f$ Electrons
item.dc:creator: Zhongjie Wang, Rui Song, Yupeng Jiang, Qidong Sun, Meng Zhao, Lifeng Yin, Jian Shen, and Chunlei Gao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Gate-Tunable Spectrum and Charge Dispersion Mitigation in a Graphene Superconducting Qubit
item.description:
Author(s): Nicolas Aparicio, Simon Messelot, Edgar Bonet-Orozco, Eric Eyraud, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Johann Coraux, and Julien Renard

Controlling the energy spectrum of quantum-coherent superconducting circuits, i.e., the energies of excited states, the circuit anharmonicity, and the states’ charge dispersion, is essential for designing performant qubits. This control is usually achieved by adjusting the circuit’s geometry. In sit…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266001] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nicolas Aparicio, Simon Messelot, Edgar Bonet-Orozco, Eric Eyraud, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Johann Coraux, and Julien Renard

Controlling the energy spectrum of quantum-coherent superconducting circuits, i.e., the energies of excited states, the circuit anharmonicity, and the states’ charge dispersion, is essential for designing performant qubits. This control is usually achieved by adjusting the circuit’s geometry. In sit…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266001] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.dc:title: Gate-Tunable Spectrum and Charge Dispersion Mitigation in a Graphene Superconducting Qubit
item.dc:creator: Nicolas Aparicio, Simon Messelot, Edgar Bonet-Orozco, Eric Eyraud, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Johann Coraux, and Julien Renard

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Double Supersolid Phase in a Bosonic $t\text{−}J\text{−}V$ Model with Rydberg Atoms
item.description:
Author(s): Kuangjie Chen, Yang Qi, Zheng Yan, and Xiaopeng Li

Recent advances in Rydberg tweezer arrays bring novel opportunities for programmable quantum simulations beyond previous capabilities. In this Letter, we investigate a bosonic $t\text{−}J\text{−}V$ model currently realized with Rydberg atoms. Through large-scale quantum Monte Carlo simulations, we u…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266003] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kuangjie Chen, Yang Qi, Zheng Yan, and Xiaopeng Li

Recent advances in Rydberg tweezer arrays bring novel opportunities for programmable quantum simulations beyond previous capabilities. In this Letter, we investigate a bosonic t−J−V model currently realized with Rydberg atoms. Through large-scale quantum Monte Carlo simulations, we uncover an emerge…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266003] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.dc:title: Double Supersolid Phase in a Bosonic $t\text{−}J\text{−}V$ Model with Rydberg Atoms
item.dc:creator: Kuangjie Chen, Yang Qi, Zheng Yan, and Xiaopeng Li
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Unconventional Superconductivity Mediated by Exciton Density Wave Fluctuations
item.description:
Author(s): Ajesh Kumar, Adarsh S. Patri, and T. Senthil

Synthetic platforms afford an unparalleled degree of controllability in realizing strongly correlated phases of matter. In this Letter, we study the possibility of electrically tunable exciton-mediated superconductivity arising in charge-imbalanced bilayer semiconductors. Focusing on the case of a b…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266501] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ajesh Kumar, Adarsh S. Patri, and T. Senthil

Synthetic platforms afford an unparalleled degree of controllability in realizing strongly correlated phases of matter. In this Letter, we study the possibility of electrically tunable exciton-mediated superconductivity arising in charge-imbalanced bilayer semiconductors. Focusing on the case of a b…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266501] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.dc:title: Unconventional Superconductivity Mediated by Exciton Density Wave Fluctuations
item.dc:creator: Ajesh Kumar, Adarsh S. Patri, and T. Senthil

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Loss-Induced Bulk-Boundary Detachment in a Photonic Chern Insulator
item.description:
Author(s): Yan-Chen Zhou, Hua-Shan Lai, Ze-Qun Sun, Xiao-Chen Sun, Jian-Lan Xie, Cheng He, and Yan-Feng Chen

Chiral edge states (CESs) in Chern insulators enable one-way propagation without backscattering loss. However, they are usually limited to narrow bandwidths within independent band gaps, and the intrinsic loss is generally viewed as a detrimental factor for transport. Here, leveraging non-Hermitian …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266602] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yan-Chen Zhou, Hua-Shan Lai, Ze-Qun Sun, Xiao-Chen Sun, Jian-Lan Xie, Cheng He, and Yan-Feng Chen

Chiral edge states (CESs) in Chern insulators enable one-way propagation without backscattering loss. However, they are usually limited to narrow bandwidths within independent band gaps, and the intrinsic loss is generally viewed as a detrimental factor for transport. Here, leveraging non-Hermitian …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266602] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.dc:title: Loss-Induced Bulk-Boundary Detachment in a Photonic Chern Insulator
item.dc:creator: Yan-Chen Zhou, Hua-Shan Lai, Ze-Qun Sun, Xiao-Chen Sun, Jian-Lan Xie, Cheng He, and Yan-Feng Chen
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Spin Inversion Enforced by Crystal Symmetry in Ferroelastic Altermagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Yuqiang Huang, Chenqiang Hua, Runzhang Xu, Junwei Liu, Yi Zheng, and Yunhao Lu

The realm of spintronics has witnessed a profound surge in fascination towards altermagnetism, fueled by groundbreaking predictions and a myriad of promising applications. Here, we propose a novel multiferroic mechanism between ferroelasticity and altermagnetism based on symmetry analysis. Through f…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266701] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuqiang Huang, Chenqiang Hua, Runzhang Xu, Junwei Liu, Yi Zheng, and Yunhao Lu

The realm of spintronics has witnessed a profound surge in fascination towards altermagnetism, fueled by groundbreaking predictions and a myriad of promising applications. Here, we propose a novel multiferroic mechanism between ferroelasticity and altermagnetism based on symmetry analysis. Through f…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266701] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.dc:title: Spin Inversion Enforced by Crystal Symmetry in Ferroelastic Altermagnets
item.dc:creator: Yuqiang Huang, Chenqiang Hua, Runzhang Xu, Junwei Liu, Yi Zheng, and Yunhao Lu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Observation and Control of Potential-Dependent Surface-State Formation at a Semiconductor-Electrolyte Interface via Optical Anisotropy
item.description:
Author(s): Marco Flieg, Margot Guidat, and Matthias M. May

Surface states at the InP semiconductor-electrolyte interface can be switched on or off with applied potential, and detection of this phenomenon is allowed by optical anisotropy.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 268001] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Marco Flieg, Margot Guidat, and Matthias M. May

Surface states at the InP semiconductor-electrolyte interface can be switched on or off with applied potential, and detection of this phenomenon is allowed by optical anisotropy.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 268001] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.dc:title: Observation and Control of Potential-Dependent Surface-State Formation at a Semiconductor-Electrolyte Interface via Optical Anisotropy
item.dc:creator: Marco Flieg, Margot Guidat, and Matthias M. May
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Polymers, Chemical Physics, Soft Matter, and Biological Physics
item.prism:section: Polymers, Chemical Physics, Soft Matter, and Biological Physics

item.title: Demonstration of Discrete-Time Quantum Walks and Observation of Topological Edge States in a Superconducting Qutrit Chain
item.description:
Author(s): Kun Zhou, Jian-Wen Xu, Qi-Ping Su, Yu Zhang, Xiang-Min Yu, Zhuang Ma, Han-Yu Zhang, Hong-Yi Shi, Wen Zheng, Shuyi Pan, Yihao Kang, Zhiguo Huang, Chui-Ping Yang, Shao-Xiong Li, and Yang Yu

A qutrit-based realization of a discrete-time quantum walk overcomes the scalability challenge and demonstrates the generation of topological edge states.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 250601] Published Fri Dec 19, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kun Zhou, Jian-Wen Xu, Qi-Ping Su, Yu Zhang, Xiang-Min Yu, Zhuang Ma, Han-Yu Zhang, Hong-Yi Shi, Wen Zheng, Shuyi Pan, Yihao Kang, Zhiguo Huang, Chui-Ping Yang, Shao-Xiong Li, and Yang Yu

A qutrit-based realization of a discrete-time quantum walk overcomes the scalability challenge and demonstrates the generation of topological edge states.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 250601] Published Fri Dec 19, 2025
item.dc:title: Demonstration of Discrete-Time Quantum Walks and Observation of Topological Edge States in a Superconducting Qutrit Chain
item.dc:creator: Kun Zhou, Jian-Wen Xu, Qi-Ping Su, Yu Zhang, Xiang-Min Yu, Zhuang Ma, Han-Yu Zhang, Hong-Yi Shi, Wen Zheng, Shuyi Pan, Yihao Kang, Zhiguo Huang, Chui-Ping Yang, Shao-Xiong Li, and Yang Yu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Overcoming Frequency Resolution Limits Using a Solid-State Spin Quantum Sensor
item.description:
Author(s): Qingyun Cao, Genko T. Genov, Yaoming Chu, Jianming Cai, Yu Liu, Alex Retzker, and Fedor Jelezko

The ability to determine precisely the separation of two frequencies is fundamental to spectroscopy, yet the resolution limit poses a critical challenge: distinguishing two incoherent signals becomes impossible when their frequencies are sufficiently close. Here, we demonstrate a simple and powerful…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 250806] Published Fri Dec 19, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Qingyun Cao, Genko T. Genov, Yaoming Chu, Jianming Cai, Yu Liu, Alex Retzker, and Fedor Jelezko

The ability to determine precisely the separation of two frequencies is fundamental to spectroscopy, yet the resolution limit poses a critical challenge: distinguishing two incoherent signals becomes impossible when their frequencies are sufficiently close. Here, we demonstrate a simple and powerful…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 250806] Published Fri Dec 19, 2025
item.dc:title: Overcoming Frequency Resolution Limits Using a Solid-State Spin Quantum Sensor
item.dc:creator: Qingyun Cao, Genko T. Genov, Yaoming Chu, Jianming Cai, Yu Liu, Alex Retzker, and Fedor Jelezko
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Evidence of Surface Interlayer Dimerization in the Commensurate Charge Density Wave Phase of $1T\text{−}{\mathrm{TaSe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Niccolò Mignani, Alberto Crepaldi, Luca Moreschini, Aaron Bostwick, Chris Jozwiak, Eli Rotenberg, Simon Crampin, Enrico Da Como, and Ettore Carpene

Van der Waals layered materials offer unprecedented opportunities to tune electronic properties by controlling, for instance, the number of layers or their mutual twist angle. However, the translational degree of freedom has not been given proportionate attention. Within the wide family of transitio…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256203] Published Fri Dec 19, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Niccolò Mignani, Alberto Crepaldi, Luca Moreschini, Aaron Bostwick, Chris Jozwiak, Eli Rotenberg, Simon Crampin, Enrico Da Como, and Ettore Carpene

Van der Waals layered materials offer unprecedented opportunities to tune electronic properties by controlling, for instance, the number of layers or their mutual twist angle. However, the translational degree of freedom has not been given proportionate attention. Within the wide family of transitio…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256203] Published Fri Dec 19, 2025
item.dc:title: Evidence of Surface Interlayer Dimerization in the Commensurate Charge Density Wave Phase of $1T\text{−}{\mathrm{TaSe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Niccolò Mignani, Alberto Crepaldi, Luca Moreschini, Aaron Bostwick, Chris Jozwiak, Eli Rotenberg, Simon Crampin, Enrico Da Como, and Ettore Carpene
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Proper Definition of Intrinsic Nonlinear Current
item.description:
Author(s): Cong Xiao, Jin Cao, Qian Niu, and Shengyuan A. Yang

We show that the three commonly employed approaches that define the same dc (or low-frequency) intrinsic linear anomalous Hall response actually lead to different results for intrinsic nonlinear transport. The difference is due to an intrinsic anomalous distribution (IAD). It originates from a nonli…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256306] Published Fri Dec 19, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Cong Xiao, Jin Cao, Qian Niu, and Shengyuan A. Yang

We show that the three commonly employed approaches that define the same dc (or low-frequency) intrinsic linear anomalous Hall response actually lead to different results for intrinsic nonlinear transport. The difference is due to an intrinsic anomalous distribution (IAD). It originates from a nonli…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256306] Published Fri Dec 19, 2025
item.dc:title: Proper Definition of Intrinsic Nonlinear Current
item.dc:creator: Cong Xiao, Jin Cao, Qian Niu, and Shengyuan A. Yang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Projectively Implemented Altermagnetism in an Exactly Solvable Quantum Spin Liquid
item.description:
Author(s): Avedis Neehus, Achim Rosch, Johannes Knolle, and Urban F. P. Seifert

Altermagnets are a new class of symmetry-compensated magnets with large spin splittings. Here, we show that the notion of altermagnetism extends beyond the realm of Landau-type order: we study exactly solvable ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2}$ quantum spin(-orbital) liquids (QSLs), which simultaneously support mag…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256504] Published Fri Dec 19, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Avedis Neehus, Achim Rosch, Johannes Knolle, and Urban F. P. Seifert

Altermagnets are a new class of symmetry-compensated magnets with large spin splittings. Here, we show that the notion of altermagnetism extends beyond the realm of Landau-type order: we study exactly solvable Z2 quantum spin(-orbital) liquids (QSLs), which simultaneously support magnetic long-range…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256504] Published Fri Dec 19, 2025
item.dc:title: Projectively Implemented Altermagnetism in an Exactly Solvable Quantum Spin Liquid
item.dc:creator: Avedis Neehus, Achim Rosch, Johannes Knolle, and Urban F. P. Seifert
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Bragg Coherent Modulation Imaging of Highly Strained Nanocrystals
item.description:
Author(s): Jiangtao Zhao, Ewen Bellec, Marie-Ingrid Richard, Linus Pithan, Ivan A. Vartanyants, Fucai Zhang, Tobias Schülli, and Steven J. Leake

Researchers have demonstrated a technique for measuring lattice distortions that are too big for conventional methods to handle.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256101] Published Thu Dec 18, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiangtao Zhao, Ewen Bellec, Marie-Ingrid Richard, Linus Pithan, Ivan A. Vartanyants, Fucai Zhang, Tobias Schülli, and Steven J. Leake

Researchers have demonstrated a technique for measuring lattice distortions that are too big for conventional methods to handle.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256101] Published Thu Dec 18, 2025
item.dc:title: Bragg Coherent Modulation Imaging of Highly Strained Nanocrystals
item.dc:creator: Jiangtao Zhao, Ewen Bellec, Marie-Ingrid Richard, Linus Pithan, Ivan A. Vartanyants, Fucai Zhang, Tobias Schülli, and Steven J. Leake

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Surface-State Engineering for Generation of Nonlinear Charge and Spin Photocurrents
item.description:
Author(s): Javier Sivianes, Peio Garcia-Goiricelaya, Daniel Hernangómez-Pérez, and Julen Ibañez-Azpiroz

We systematically explore the generation of nonlinear charge and spin photocurrents using spin-orbit split surface states. This mechanism enables net DC flow along the surface plane even in centrosymmetric bulk environments like the Rashba prototype Au(111), where we characterize the main quadratic …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256201] Published Thu Dec 18, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Javier Sivianes, Peio Garcia-Goiricelaya, Daniel Hernangómez-Pérez, and Julen Ibañez-Azpiroz

We systematically explore the generation of nonlinear charge and spin photocurrents using spin-orbit split surface states. This mechanism enables net DC flow along the surface plane even in centrosymmetric bulk environments like the Rashba prototype Au(111), where we characterize the main quadratic …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256201] Published Thu Dec 18, 2025
item.dc:title: Surface-State Engineering for Generation of Nonlinear Charge and Spin Photocurrents
item.dc:creator: Javier Sivianes, Peio Garcia-Goiricelaya, Daniel Hernangómez-Pérez, and Julen Ibañez-Azpiroz
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Critical States of Fermions with ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2}$ Flux Disorder
item.description:
Author(s): Hiranmay Das, Naba P. Nayak, Soumya Bera, and Vijay B. Shenoy

We investigate the physics of fermions on a square lattice with $π$ flux, subjected to disordered random ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2}$ gauge fields that arise from flux defects, i.e., plaquettes with zero flux. At half filling, where the system possesses BDI symmetry, we show that a new class of critical state…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256305] Published Thu Dec 18, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hiranmay Das, Naba P. Nayak, Soumya Bera, and Vijay B. Shenoy

We investigate the physics of fermions on a square lattice with π flux, subjected to disordered random Z2 gauge fields that arise from flux defects, i.e., plaquettes with zero flux. At half filling, where the system possesses BDI symmetry, we show that a new class of critical states is realized, wit…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256305] Published Thu Dec 18, 2025
item.dc:title: Critical States of Fermions with ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2}$ Flux Disorder
item.dc:creator: Hiranmay Das, Naba P. Nayak, Soumya Bera, and Vijay B. Shenoy
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantum Geometry in Phonon-Mediated Optical Responses
item.description:
Author(s): Jiaming Hu, Wenbin Li, Zhichao Guo, Hua Wang, and Kai Chang

Quantum geometry is crucial for understanding intricate condensed matter systems, governing transport phenomena and optical responses. However, traditional studies of quantum geometry predominantly consider a static crystal lattice, focusing exclusively on the pure-electronic quantum geometry of the…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256404] Published Thu Dec 18, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiaming Hu, Wenbin Li, Zhichao Guo, Hua Wang, and Kai Chang

Quantum geometry is crucial for understanding intricate condensed matter systems, governing transport phenomena and optical responses. However, traditional studies of quantum geometry predominantly consider a static crystal lattice, focusing exclusively on the pure-electronic quantum geometry of the…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256404] Published Thu Dec 18, 2025
item.dc:title: Quantum Geometry in Phonon-Mediated Optical Responses
item.dc:creator: Jiaming Hu, Wenbin Li, Zhichao Guo, Hua Wang, and Kai Chang

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantum Geometric Origin of Strain-Induced Ferroelectric Phase Transitions
item.description:
Author(s): Jiaming Hu, Ziye Zhu, Yubo Yuan, Hua Wang, and Kai Chang

Strain-regulated ferroelectric (FE) materials have long attracted significant attention due to their diverse applications. While soft-phonon theory and the (pseudo) Jahn-Teller effect have achieved considerable success in providing phenomenological descriptions and general understanding, the detaile…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256405] Published Thu Dec 18, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiaming Hu, Ziye Zhu, Yubo Yuan, Hua Wang, and Kai Chang

Strain-regulated ferroelectric (FE) materials have long attracted significant attention due to their diverse applications. While soft-phonon theory and the (pseudo) Jahn-Teller effect have achieved considerable success in providing phenomenological descriptions and general understanding, the detaile…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256405] Published Thu Dec 18, 2025
item.dc:title: Quantum Geometric Origin of Strain-Induced Ferroelectric Phase Transitions
item.dc:creator: Jiaming Hu, Ziye Zhu, Yubo Yuan, Hua Wang, and Kai Chang

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Emergent Dispersive Multipolar Excitations in ${\mathrm{NaErSe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Zheng Zhang, Mingfang Shu, Mingtai Xie, Weizhen Zhuo, Yanzhen Cai, Christian Balz, Jianting Ji, Feng Jin, Jie Ma, and Qingming Zhang

In most condensed-matter systems, local and collective excitations remain decoupled due to their distinct energy scales. Here, we identify the coupled local-collective excitations in the triangular antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{NaErSe}}_{2}$, by combining neutron spectroscopy with a total angular moment…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256503] Published Thu Dec 18, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zheng Zhang, Mingfang Shu, Mingtai Xie, Weizhen Zhuo, Yanzhen Cai, Christian Balz, Jianting Ji, Feng Jin, Jie Ma, and Qingming Zhang

In most condensed-matter systems, local and collective excitations remain decoupled due to their distinct energy scales. Here, we identify the coupled local-collective excitations in the triangular antiferromagnet NaErSe2, by combining neutron spectroscopy with a total angular momentum modeling. The…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256503] Published Thu Dec 18, 2025
item.dc:title: Emergent Dispersive Multipolar Excitations in ${\mathrm{NaErSe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Zheng Zhang, Mingfang Shu, Mingtai Xie, Weizhen Zhuo, Yanzhen Cai, Christian Balz, Jianting Ji, Feng Jin, Jie Ma, and Qingming Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Essay: Generalized Landau Paradigm for Quantum Phases and Phase Transitions
item.description:
Author(s): Xie Chen

In this final forward-looking Essay of the year, Xie Chen discusses how the generalized Landau principle reframes beyond-Landau phases and transitions as symmetry-breaking phases of potentially generalized symmetries and symmetry-breaking transitions, providing a more systematic framework for their study.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 250001] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xie Chen

In this final forward-looking Essay of the year, Xie Chen discusses how the generalized Landau principle reframes beyond-Landau phases and transitions as symmetry-breaking phases of potentially generalized symmetries and symmetry-breaking transitions, providing a more systematic framework for their study.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 250001] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Essay: Generalized Landau Paradigm for Quantum Phases and Phase Transitions
item.dc:creator: Xie Chen
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Editorials, Essays, and Announcements

item.title: Emergent Disorder and Sub-ballistic Dynamics in Quantum Simulations of the Ising Model Using Rydberg Atom Arrays
item.description:
Author(s): Ceren B. Dağ, Hanzhen Ma, P. Myles Eugenio, Fang Fang, and Susanne F. Yelin

Rydberg atom arrays with van der Waals interactions provide a controllable path to quantum simulate the locally connected transverse-field Ising model (TFIM), a prototypical model in statistical mechanics. Remotely operating the publicly accessible Aquila Rydberg atom array, we experimentally invest…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 250403] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ceren B. Dağ, Hanzhen Ma, P. Myles Eugenio, Fang Fang, and Susanne F. Yelin

Rydberg atom arrays with van der Waals interactions provide a controllable path to quantum simulate the locally connected transverse-field Ising model (TFIM), a prototypical model in statistical mechanics. Remotely operating the publicly accessible Aquila Rydberg atom array, we experimentally invest…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 250403] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Emergent Disorder and Sub-ballistic Dynamics in Quantum Simulations of the Ising Model Using Rydberg Atom Arrays
item.dc:creator: Ceren B. Dağ, Hanzhen Ma, P. Myles Eugenio, Fang Fang, and Susanne F. Yelin
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Anyonization of Bosons in One Dimension: An Effective Swap Model
item.description:
Author(s): Botao Wang, Amit Vashisht, Yanliang Guo, Sudipta Dhar, Manuele Landini, Hanns-Christoph Nägerl, and Nathan Goldman

Anyons emerge as elementary excitations in low-dimensional quantum systems and exhibit behavior distinct from bosons or fermions. In one dimension, anyons can arise from unconventional scattering processes or density-dependent hopping on a lattice. Here, we introduce a novel framework for realizing …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 253403] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Botao Wang, Amit Vashisht, Yanliang Guo, Sudipta Dhar, Manuele Landini, Hanns-Christoph Nägerl, and Nathan Goldman

Anyons emerge as elementary excitations in low-dimensional quantum systems and exhibit behavior distinct from bosons or fermions. In one dimension, anyons can arise from unconventional scattering processes or density-dependent hopping on a lattice. Here, we introduce a novel framework for realizing …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 253403] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Anyonization of Bosons in One Dimension: An Effective Swap Model
item.dc:creator: Botao Wang, Amit Vashisht, Yanliang Guo, Sudipta Dhar, Manuele Landini, Hanns-Christoph Nägerl, and Nathan Goldman

item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Superfluid Dome in the Spatially Modulated Two-Dimensional XY Model
item.description:
Author(s): Feng-Feng Song, Aditya Chugh, Hanggai Nuomin, Naoki Kawashima, and Alexander Wietek

In strongly correlated electron systems, superconductivity and charge density waves often coexist in close proximity, suggesting a deeper relationship between these competing phases. Recent research indicates that these orders can intertwine, with the superconducting order parameter coupling to modu…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256001] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Feng-Feng Song, Aditya Chugh, Hanggai Nuomin, Naoki Kawashima, and Alexander Wietek

In strongly correlated electron systems, superconductivity and charge density waves often coexist in close proximity, suggesting a deeper relationship between these competing phases. Recent research indicates that these orders can intertwine, with the superconducting order parameter coupling to modu…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256001] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Superfluid Dome in the Spatially Modulated Two-Dimensional XY Model
item.dc:creator: Feng-Feng Song, Aditya Chugh, Hanggai Nuomin, Naoki Kawashima, and Alexander Wietek
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Phonons in Epitaxial ${\mathrm{DySi}}_{2}$: From the Bulk to Self-Organized Nanoislands and Nanowires
item.description:
Author(s): Svetoslav Stankov, Przemysław Piekarz, Anja Seiler, Dániel G. Merkel, Olga Bauder, Ramu Pradip, Tilo G. Baumbach, Aleksandr I. Chumakov, and Rudolf Rüffer

Using nuclear inelastic scattering on $^{161}\mathrm{Dy}$, we determined the Dy-partial phonon density of states of epitaxial ${\mathrm{DySi}}_{2}$ in bulk and self-organized nanoislands and nanowires (NWs) on vicinal Si(001) surface. Compared to the bulk, the nanoislands exhibit softening of the cr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256202] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Svetoslav Stankov, Przemysław Piekarz, Anja Seiler, Dániel G. Merkel, Olga Bauder, Ramu Pradip, Tilo G. Baumbach, Aleksandr I. Chumakov, and Rudolf Rüffer

Using nuclear inelastic scattering on Dy161, we determined the Dy-partial phonon density of states of epitaxial DySi2 in bulk and self-organized nanoislands and nanowires (NWs) on vicinal Si(001) surface. Compared to the bulk, the nanoislands exhibit softening of the crystal lattice, which is furthe…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256202] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Phonons in Epitaxial ${\mathrm{DySi}}_{2}$: From the Bulk to Self-Organized Nanoislands and Nanowires
item.dc:creator: Svetoslav Stankov, Przemysław Piekarz, Anja Seiler, Dániel G. Merkel, Olga Bauder, Ramu Pradip, Tilo G. Baumbach, Aleksandr I. Chumakov, and Rudolf Rüffer
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Intrinsic High Chern Numbers in Two-Dimensional ${\mathrm{M}}_{2}{\mathrm{X}}_{2}$ Materials
item.description:
Author(s): Zujian Dai, Xudong Zhu, and Lixin He

Despite sharing a common lattice structure, monolayer ${\mathrm{M}}_{2}{\mathrm{X}}_{2}$ compounds realize quantum anomalous Hall phases with distinct Chern numbers, a striking phenomenon that has not been fully explored. Combining first-principles calculations with symmetry analysis and tight-bindi…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256401] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zujian Dai, Xudong Zhu, and Lixin He

Despite sharing a common lattice structure, monolayer M2X2 compounds realize quantum anomalous Hall phases with distinct Chern numbers, a striking phenomenon that has not been fully explored. Combining first-principles calculations with symmetry analysis and tight-binding models, we identify two gen…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256401] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Intrinsic High Chern Numbers in Two-Dimensional ${\mathrm{M}}_{2}{\mathrm{X}}_{2}$ Materials
item.dc:creator: Zujian Dai, Xudong Zhu, and Lixin He
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Importance of Nonadiabatic Effects in Kohn Anomalies in 1D Metals
item.description:
Author(s): Enrico Marazzi, Samuel Poncé, Jean-Christophe Charlier, and Gian-Marco Rignanese

Kohn anomalies are kinks or dips in phonon dispersions which are pronounced in low-dimensional materials. We investigate the effects of nonadiabatic phonon self-energy on Kohn anomalies in one-dimensional metals by developing a model that analyzes how the adiabatic phonon frequency, electron effecti…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256402] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Enrico Marazzi, Samuel Poncé, Jean-Christophe Charlier, and Gian-Marco Rignanese

Kohn anomalies are kinks or dips in phonon dispersions which are pronounced in low-dimensional materials. We investigate the effects of nonadiabatic phonon self-energy on Kohn anomalies in one-dimensional metals by developing a model that analyzes how the adiabatic phonon frequency, electron effecti…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256402] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Importance of Nonadiabatic Effects in Kohn Anomalies in 1D Metals
item.dc:creator: Enrico Marazzi, Samuel Poncé, Jean-Christophe Charlier, and Gian-Marco Rignanese
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Chiral Wigner Crystal Phases Induced by Berry Curvature
item.description:
Author(s): Sandeep Joy, Leonid Levitov, and Brian Skinner

We study how Berry phase affects the Wigner crystal (WC) state of a two-dimensional electron system. We consider first a model of Bernal bilayer graphene with a perpendicular displacement field, and we show that Berry curvature leads to a new kind of WC state in which the electrons acquire a spontan…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256502] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sandeep Joy, Leonid Levitov, and Brian Skinner

We study how Berry phase affects the Wigner crystal (WC) state of a two-dimensional electron system. We consider first a model of Bernal bilayer graphene with a perpendicular displacement field, and we show that Berry curvature leads to a new kind of WC state in which the electrons acquire a spontan…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256502] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Chiral Wigner Crystal Phases Induced by Berry Curvature
item.dc:creator: Sandeep Joy, Leonid Levitov, and Brian Skinner

item.title: Quantized Crystalline-Electromagnetic Responses in Insulators
item.description:
Author(s): Sachin Vaidya, André Grossi Fonseca, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Taylor L. Hughes, and Marin Soljačić

Nonsymmorphic momentum-space symmetries quantize electromagnetic-crystalline responses in insulators.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256602] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025

Author(s): Sachin Vaidya, André Grossi Fonseca, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Taylor L. Hughes, and Marin Soljačić

Nonsymmorphic momentum-space symmetries quantize electromagnetic-crystalline responses in insulators.

item.title: Fractional Topological States in Rhombohedral Multilayer Graphene Modulated by Kagome Superlattice
item.description:
Author(s): Yanran Shi, Bo Xie, Fengfan Ren, Xinyu Cai, Zhongqing Guo, Qiao Li, Xin Lu, Nicolas Regnault, Zhongkai Liu, and Jianpeng Liu

Rhombohedral multilayer graphene coupled with a patterned kagome superlattice produces various zero-magnetic-field fractional topological states.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256603] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yanran Shi, Bo Xie, Fengfan Ren, Xinyu Cai, Zhongqing Guo, Qiao Li, Xin Lu, Nicolas Regnault, Zhongkai Liu, and Jianpeng Liu

Rhombohedral multilayer graphene coupled with a patterned kagome superlattice produces various zero-magnetic-field fractional topological states.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256603] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Two-Dimensional ${J}_{1}\text{−}{J}_{2}$ Clock Model: Enhanced Symmetries, Emergent Orders, and Landau-Incompatible Transitions
item.description:
Author(s): Pulloor Kuttanikkad Vishnu, Abhishodh Prakash, Rajesh Narayanan, and Titas Chanda

We present a comprehensive study on the frustrated ${J}_{1}\text{−}{J}_{2}$ classical $q$-state clock model with even $q>4$ on a two-dimensional square lattice, revealing a rich ensemble of phases driven by competing interactions. In the unfrustrated regime (${J}_{1}>2{J}_{2}$), the model repr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256703] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pulloor Kuttanikkad Vishnu, Abhishodh Prakash, Rajesh Narayanan, and Titas Chanda

We present a comprehensive study on the frustrated J1−J2 classical q-state clock model with even q>4 on a two-dimensional square lattice, revealing a rich ensemble of phases driven by competing interactions. In the unfrustrated regime (J1>2J2), the model reproduces the standard clock model phe…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256703] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Two-Dimensional ${J}_{1}\text{−}{J}_{2}$ Clock Model: Enhanced Symmetries, Emergent Orders, and Landau-Incompatible Transitions
item.dc:creator: Pulloor Kuttanikkad Vishnu, Abhishodh Prakash, Rajesh Narayanan, and Titas Chanda
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Electrically Switchable Nonrelativistic Zeeman Spin Splittings in Collinear Antiferromagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Longju Yu, Hong Jian Zhao, Laurent Bellaiche, and Yanming Ma

Magnetic or electrical manipulation of electronic spin is elementary for spin-based logic, computing, and memory, where the latter is a low-power manipulation scheme. Rashba-like spin splittings stemming from spin-orbit interaction (SOI) enable electric-field manipulation of spin, but the relativist…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256704] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Longju Yu, Hong Jian Zhao, Laurent Bellaiche, and Yanming Ma

Magnetic or electrical manipulation of electronic spin is elementary for spin-based logic, computing, and memory, where the latter is a low-power manipulation scheme. Rashba-like spin splittings stemming from spin-orbit interaction (SOI) enable electric-field manipulation of spin, but the relativist…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256704] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Electrically Switchable Nonrelativistic Zeeman Spin Splittings in Collinear Antiferromagnets
item.dc:creator: Longju Yu, Hong Jian Zhao, Laurent Bellaiche, and Yanming Ma
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials
